question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply crafted JVM system property arguments via environment variable injection to override `SpringApplication.run()` startup properties (e.g., `--spring.datasource.url`), causing the application to connect to an attacker-controlled database and exfiltrate credentials?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unvalidated `args` parameter passed to `SpringApplication.run(RestJavaApplication.class, args)` to inject `--spring.profiles.active=attacker` and activate a malicious profile that overrides security beans, leading to authentication bypass?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `--management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=*` as a startup argument to `SpringApplication.run()`, enabling all Spring Boot Actuator endpoints and allowing unauthenticated access to `/actuator/env`, `/actuator/shutdown`, or `/actuator/heapdump`?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration triggered by `SpringApplication.run()` to discover and access an exposed `/actuator/beans` endpoint, enumerating all Spring beans including sensitive configuration beans from `@Import(CommonConfiguration.class)`, leading to information disclosure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the `@SpringBootApplication` component scan of the `org.hiero.mirror.restjava` package to trigger auto-configuration of an insecure default bean (e.g., H2 console, default security config) that was not intended to be active, leading to unauthorized data access?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` annotation's default permissive auto-configuration to access an unauthenticated Spring Boot `/actuator/loggers` endpoint and change log levels to DEBUG, causing sensitive data (credentials, tokens) to be written to logs they can later read?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access an exposed `/actuator/env` endpoint, reading environment variables that include database passwords injected into `CommonConfiguration`'s `dataSource()` bean, leading to credential theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user send a POST request to `/actuator/shutdown` (auto-configured via `@SpringBootApplication`) to trigger `SpringApplication` context shutdown, causing a denial-of-service by terminating the `RestJavaApplication` process?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/heapdump`, downloading a full JVM heap dump that contains in-memory secrets (database passwords, API keys) loaded by `CommonConfiguration`?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/threaddump`, enumerating all active threads including those spawned by `@EnableAsync` and `@EnableScheduling`, leaking internal execution flow and timing information useful for further attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/metrics`, enumerating internal performance metrics that reveal database query patterns, connection pool sizes from `CommonConfiguration`'s `HikariDataSource`, and scheduled task execution times?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/configprops`, reading all `@ConfigurationProperties` beans registered by `CommonConfiguration`'s `@ConfigurationPropertiesScan`, potentially exposing sensitive configuration values?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/mappings`, enumerating all HTTP request mappings in the `RestJavaApplication`, revealing hidden or undocumented API endpoints that can be targeted for further exploitation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/info`, leaking application version, build metadata, and git commit information that aids in identifying known CVEs applicable to the `RestJavaApplication`?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/health`, obtaining detailed health check information including database connectivity status from `CommonConfiguration`'s `DatabaseWaiter`, revealing internal infrastructure topology?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/scheduledtasks`, enumerating all scheduled tasks registered via `@EnableScheduling`, revealing cron expressions and task names that expose internal maintenance schedules?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/caches`, enumerating all caches registered in the `RestJavaApplication` context, and then use `/actuator/caches/{cache}` DELETE to evict caches, causing cache stampede and database overload?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to access `/actuator/logfile`, reading the application log file that may contain sensitive data logged during `SpringApplication.run()` startup, including database connection strings?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to POST to `/actuator/env` and inject a new property source that overrides `spring.datasource.url` used by `CommonConfiguration`'s `dataSource()` bean, redirecting database connections to an attacker-controlled server?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@SpringBootApplication` auto-configuration to POST to `/actuator/refresh` (if Spring Cloud is on classpath), forcing a context refresh that re-reads environment variables and potentially picks up attacker-injected configuration, leading to application misconfiguration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Class: RestJavaApplication] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@EnableAsync` annotation on `RestJavaApplication` to flood the default async task executor thread pool with long-running requests, exhausting all available threads and causing a denial-of-service for all async operations in the application?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Class: RestJavaApplication] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@EnableAsync` annotation's default unbounded task queue to submit an unlimited number of async tasks via public API endpoints, causing unbounded memory growth in the task queue and eventually triggering an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Class: RestJavaApplication] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@EnableAsync` annotation's default `SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor` (which creates a new thread per task if no custom executor is configured) to trigger unbounded thread creation via repeated API calls, exhausting JVM thread limits and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Class: RestJavaApplication] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@EnableAsync` annotation to trigger async methods that swallow exceptions silently (no `AsyncUncaughtExceptionHandler` configured), causing silent data corruption or incomplete operations that are never retried, leading to data integrity violations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/RestJavaApplication.java -> Scope: '] [Class: RestJavaApplication] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@EnableAsync` annotation's thread pool to cause thread starvation by submitting requests that hold async threads indefinitely (e.g., via slow HTTP connections), preventing legitimate async tasks from executing and causing application-wide degradation?
